revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]]
{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|21|21.8|N|139|33|16.0|E}}}} '''Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster''' (ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群) is an ancient {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in {{ill|Kōden-chō, Yokohama|ja|公田町 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōden-chō, Yokohama|1=Kuden-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20044014}}, {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qid=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama|1=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the many tomb clusters that once existed along the {{ill|Kashio River|ja|㹨川|1=Itachi River|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashio River|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11259475}}, which historically contained approximately 20 clusters with around 200 individual tombs, collectively known as the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster is located in a forested area along the {{ill|Arai-sawa River|ja|洗井沢川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arai-sawa River|1=Araisawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q100902720}}, a tributary of the Kashio River. A total of 12 tombs have been identified at the site. These tombs are characterized as {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}}, dating back to the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. Their defining feature is the presence of a "coffin chamber" (棺室).<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
According to local legend, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, a samurai named "Hikoshirō" fled to this area and hid inside one of the cave tombs. As a result, the area came to be known as "Hikoshigaya" (ひこしヶ谷).<ref>{{ill|Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division|ja|栄区地域振興課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Sakae Ward Local History Handbook'' (5th edition, 2015), p. 46.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == === Reference Works === * {{ill|Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division|ja|栄区地域振興課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Sakae Ward Local History Handbook'' (5th edition), Sakae Ward Regional Promotion Division, 2015. * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929301}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (2015).
=== Related Works === * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Yokoanabo Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (1964).
== See Also == * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}}
{{coord|35|21|21.8|N|139|33|16.0|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Tombs) - 23 tombs == ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoanabos|en|Kashiya Yokoanabos|qid=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Yokoanabos|1=Kashiya Yokoanabos}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|en|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|qid=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Yokoanabos|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoanabos|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|qid=Q11419962|lt=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|1=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokoanabos.
*
